rationale: Author-of-record, round 2: the c9f54530 delta stays entirely within Bead-1 scope and the consolidated-round-1.md mandate. It touches exactly the three sanctioned files (create.go, create_test.go, interfaces.md), tally.go is verified untouched (mandate item 4 explicitly forbade changing it), and every hunk maps to one of the six must-items — no seventh change, no new feature, no plan-step reinterpretation. The marker-detection refinement (genuine-marker-only, fence-aware) does not reinterpret plan Step 2; it more faithfully serves Step 2's 'replace only the delimited region' + fail-closed-on-corrupt contract. All four original Acceptance Criteria are still delivered and green: AC1/AC2 (co-bump + one-write + verdict-file/body preservation) via the unchanged registration/marker tests; AC3 (schema doc consistent with constants, extracted from the doc's own examples) now section-scoped and stronger, still naming all three enum literals + hard_block; AC4 (config-free leaf) holds — create.go's only new import is stdlib regexp. Both carry-only non-blockers were carried, not fixed (crash-window noted in docstring only; no temp+rename; S3 B4/B5 notes deferred). Full package passes; go build ./... green; git clean.
findings: Scope-creep guard clean: git diff --name-only 17a2ed28..c9f54530 = exactly interfaces.md + create.go + create_test.go; tally.go confirmed TALLY_UNTOUCHED, honoring consolidated item 4's 'do NOT change tally.go' constraint and the one-commit/three-file fix-author constraint.

Item 1 (G1.1) in-scope: scanNonFenceLines + findMarkerLines count only a marker alone on its line at column 0, outside fenced code blocks; fenced_quote_of_marker_preserved test proves a body quoting the marker re-panels and survives byte-identical. This refines, and does not reinterpret, plan Step 2 (replace only the delimited region) — the byte-preservation guarantee is strengthened, not changed.

Item 2 (G1.2) in-scope: markerLookalikeRE + hasMarkerLookalike reject a whitespace-mangled marker as corrupt (writing neither file) instead of silently prepending a second header. Reject-as-corrupt is the mandate's recommended choice and is consistent with plan Step 2's existing fail-closed corrupt-marker contract; whitespace_mangled_marker_rejected (doubled-spaces, tabs) pins it.

Item 3 (F1) in-scope: TestPanelSchemaDoc_MatchesConstants now extracts only the '### Panel Artifact Schema' section (extractPanelSchemaSection, heading-bounded) and the new negative fixture TestPanelSchemaDoc_SectionScoped_RejectsRenamedNormativeName proves a normative panel.json->registration.json rename FAILS despite a Maintenance-Notes decoy. The checkPanelSchemaSection extraction is the mandated implementation shape (drive real doc + attack through one check), not creep; the round-1 pin semantics are preserved.

Item 4 (G1.3) in-scope: interfaces.md verdict-payload text now matches tally.go's real parsing (enum values are the meaningful ones; any other non-empty string is present-but-neither; empty/invalid = MISSING) with tally.go left untouched. AC3 intact — the doc still contains APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/REJECT and hard_block, and the hard_block-not-adjacent-to-'finding' regression guard still passes.

Items 5+6 (S2.1/S2.2) in-scope: the 'never hand-edited' claim is replaced with steady-state intent + the sanctioned /ms-panel-tally abandon hand-edit path; the full-struct-overwrite asymmetry (re-panel of an abandoned dir revives it) is documented in both the Create docstring and the doc and pinned by TestCreate_RepanelOfAbandonedPanelRevivesIt. Carry-only non-blockers honored: the sequential two-write crash window is noted in the docstring only (no temp+rename rework), and S3's B4/B5 integration notes are deferred to those beads (not touched here).

Regression check at c9f54530: go build ./... OK; go test ./internal/panel green; the three original named tests + two new tests all PASS; deps=$(go list -deps ./internal/panel) && ! grep internal/config exits 0 (leaf holds; new import is stdlib regexp); doc-sync grep for interfaces.md passes; git status clean.
